message:
Enhance release workflow and documentation

- Update release workflow for better automation
- Improve goreleaser configuration
- Update README with latest features
- Enhance install script

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -96,7 +96,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-6sao","title":"Epic: WezTerm session recovery & batch login automation","description":"Purpose
- Provide a first-class way to reach into existing WezTerm panes on the remote host and drive the Claude Code login flow across multiple active sessions without manual pane-by-pane typing.

Background
- When Claude Max usage is exhausted, existing in-progress sessions cannot be restarted without losing context. The only viable path is to issue /login inside each session.
- WezTerm mux already provides a CLI to list panes, read recent output, and inject text; we should leverage that to batch actions.

Scope
- Session discovery and matching (identify panes that are running Claude Code and/or showing rate-limit prompts).
- Batch /login injection + optional subscription choice and follow-up prompts.
- URL extraction from pane output to streamline browser auth.
- Optional compaction reminder injection for Claude Code panes.
- Structured logging, dry-run safety, and test harness coverage.

Non-Goals
- Full browser automation or remote OAuth completion (tracked in a separate epic).

Success Criteria
- Single command can detect rate-limited Claude Code panes and inject /login across them reliably.
- Operator sees a clear report of which panes were targeted, skipped, or failed.
- Tests and E2E fixtures validate behavior without a live WezTerm mux.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:26:20.207289232Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:03:54.411178762Z","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.1","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery structured logging + trace IDs","description":"Goal
- Add structured logging to the WezTerm recovery flow so pane automation is auditable and debuggable.

Details
- Log per-pane state transitions, detection matches, injections, and errors with fields: pane_id, window_id, tab_title (if available), tool, state, action, reason, timestamp.
- Emit a correlation/run ID to group events across panes.
- Support verbosity flags (info/debug) and a JSON log mode.
- Redact OAuth URLs and codes by default; only print in explicit report output.

Success
- A failed recovery can be diagnosed from logs alone without re-running the flow.","notes":"Depends on: caam-sssb (pane discovery) and caam-vpu2 (state machine).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.837047380Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:01:30.244662840Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.839060311Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:01.584581662Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:06.798771702Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.2","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery E2E script + fixtures","description":"Goal
- Provide a repeatable end-to-end smoke test for WezTerm recovery using fixtures and a mocked wezterm CLI.

Details
- Build fixture scenarios: single pane, multi-pane, mixed providers, rate-limit + login selection + URL extraction + code injection.
- Include a compaction banner scenario to validate reminder injection when enabled.
- Capture expected send-text calls and verify order/timing.
- Emit verbose, redacted logs per scenario with timestamps, pane ids, and matched patterns.
- Document a one-command run for CI or local validation.

Success
- A single command can validate the recovery flow without a live WezTerm mux.","notes":"Depends on: caam-kbif (mock CLI harness) and caam-vpu2 (recovery state machine).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:14.270225464Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:03:31.160722267Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:14.271731891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:04:40.022616496Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:18.307384892Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:22.168541814Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

All 91 tests emit structured logs: fixture name, tool, matched, reason, expected values.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:29.541102042Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:36.547532959Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:33.771976303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.4","title":"Task: OAuth URL extraction unit tests + multiline fixtures","description":"Goal
- Validate URL extraction across multi-line, wrapped, or ANSI-decorated outputs.

Details
- Fixtures include split URLs, trailing punctuation, and wrapped lines.
- Ensure full URL reconstruction without missing query params.
- Assert redaction helpers (if any) keep URLs out of logs by default.
- Emit structured test logs per fixture with extraction success and length.

Success
- URL extraction is reliable and does not leak sensitive data in logs.","notes":"Depends on: caam-qq8v (URL extraction).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:41.193677137Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:41.193677137Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.4","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:41.195208070Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.4","depends_on_id":"caam-qq8v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:46.014419658Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.5","title":"Task: login-all command tests + safety prompts","description":"Goal
- Validate caam wezterm login-all behavior across dry-run, confirmation, and selection modes.

Details
- Test --all, --match, --subscription, --yes, and --dry-run behaviors.
- Ensure no injections occur on non-matching panes unless --all is explicit.
- Verify prompt text and summary output include counts of targeted/skipped/failed panes.
- Emit structured logs in tests for pane id, action, and outcome.

Success
- Batch login is safe, predictable, and well-instrumented.","notes":"Depends on: caam-fiw4 (login-all command) and caam-kbif (mock CLI harness).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:52.217782146Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:57.914626184Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:52.219568831Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-fiw4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:00:03.607901572Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:00:07.489826956Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.6","title":"Task: Competitive research (OpenCode login handling)","description":"Goal
- Extract actionable patterns from OpenCode’s login handling to improve caam’s WezTerm recovery flow.

Details
- Review OpenCode’s auth/login recovery implementation and any terminal automation patterns.
- Identify reusable ideas: detection heuristics, prompt handling, state machines, or UX flows.
- Document findings and map them to caam changes (what to adopt, what to avoid).
- If any code changes are proposed, specify which existing beads they update and ensure tests are included.

Tests
- If integration changes are made, update or add unit + E2E tests in the relevant recovery beads.

Success
- We either confidently confirm caam’s current approach or incorporate proven patterns, with rationale documented.

@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@ on:
 
 permissions:
   contents: write
+  id-token: write
 
 jobs:
   goreleaser:
@@ -20,6 +21,9 @@ jobs:
         with:
           go-version: 'stable'
 
+      - name: Install cosign
+        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@v4.0.0
+
       - name: Run GoReleaser
         uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6
         with:
@@ -31,3 +35,25 @@ jobs:
           # Token with write access to homebrew-tap and scoop-bucket repos
           # Set this as a repository secret with repo scope
           HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+      - name: Verify release assets
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          tag="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
+          api="https://api.github.com/repos/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/releases/tags/${tag}"
+          release_json=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" "$api")
+          RELEASE_JSON="$release_json" python - <<-PY
+          	import json
+          	import os
+          	import sys
+
+          	data = json.loads(os.environ.get("RELEASE_JSON", "{}"))
+          	names = {asset.get("name") for asset in data.get("assets", [])}
+          	required = {"SHA256SUMS", "SHA256SUMS.sig"}
+          	missing = sorted(required - names)
+          	if missing:
+          	    sys.stderr.write("Missing release assets: " + ", ".join(missing) + "\n")
+          	    sys.exit(1)
+          	print("Release assets verified:", ", ".join(sorted(required)))
+          	PY

@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@ project_name: caam
 before:
   hooks:
     - go mod tidy
+    - sh -c 'mkdir -p dist && go_version=$(go env GOVERSION) && printf "{\n  \"version\": \"%s\",\n  \"commit\": \"%s\",\n  \"date\": \"%s\",\n  \"go_version\": \"%s\"\n}\n" "{{ .Version }}" "{{ .FullCommit }}" "{{ .Date }}" "$go_version" > dist/release-manifest.json'
 
 builds:
   - id: caam
@@ -37,7 +38,18 @@ archives:
         format: zip
 
 checksum:
-  name_template: "checksums.txt"
+  name_template: "SHA256SUMS"
+
+signs:
+  - id: checksum
+    cmd: cosign
+    signature: "${artifact}.sig"
+    args:
+      - "sign-blob"
+      - "--bundle=${signature}"
+      - "${artifact}"
+      - "--yes"
+    artifacts: checksum
 
 snapshot:
   version_template: "{{ incpatch .Version }}-next"
@@ -59,6 +71,8 @@ release:
     name: coding_agent_account_manager
   draft: false
   prerelease: auto
+  extra_files:
+    - glob: dist/release-manifest.json
 
 # Homebrew tap publishing
 brews:

@@ -691,6 +691,22 @@ Download the latest release for your platform:
 - [macOS ARM](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_account_manager/releases/latest/download/caam-darwin-arm64)
 - [Windows](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_account_manager/releases/latest/download/caam-windows-amd64.exe)
 
+### Verify Release Artifacts
+
+Each release ships with signed checksums:
+
+```bash
+cosign verify-blob \
+  --bundle SHA256SUMS.sig \
+  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
+  --certificate-identity "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_account_manager/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/vX.Y.Z" \
+  SHA256SUMS
+
+sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
+# macOS fallback:
+# shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS
+```
+
 ### Alternative: Install Script
 
 ```bash

@@ -299,6 +299,129 @@ if __name__ == "__main__":
 PY
 }
 
+select_named_asset() {
+    local asset_name="$1"
+    ensure_python || return 1
+
+    local release_json
+    release_json=$(cat) || return 1
+
+    CAAM_RELEASE_JSON="$release_json" "$PYTHON_CMD" - "$asset_name" <<'PY'
+import json
+import os
+import sys
+
+
+def main():
+    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
+        return 1
+    target = sys.argv[1]
+    release_json = os.environ.get("CAAM_RELEASE_JSON", "")
+    if not release_json:
+        sys.stderr.write("Missing release metadata\n")
+        return 1
+    try:
+        data = json.loads(release_json)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        sys.stderr.write(f"Failed to parse release JSON: {exc}\n")
+        return 1
+
+    assets = data.get("assets") or []
+    for asset in assets:
+        name = asset.get("name") or ""
+        if name == target:
+            url = asset.get("browser_download_url") or ""
+            if url:
+                print(url)
+                return 0
+    print("")
+    return 1
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())
+PY
+}
+
+verify_release_assets() {
+    local release_json="$1"
+    local version="$2"
+    local asset_name="$3"
+    local archive_path="$4"
+    local tmp_dir="$5"
+
+    if [ -n "${CAAM_SKIP_VERIFY:-}" ]; then
+        print_warn "Skipping release verification (CAAM_SKIP_VERIFY set)."
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local checksums_url signature_url
+    checksums_url=$(printf '%s' "$release_json" | select_named_asset "SHA256SUMS") || true
+    signature_url=$(printf '%s' "$release_json" | select_named_asset "SHA256SUMS.sig") || true
+
+    if [ -z "$checksums_url" ] || [ -z "$signature_url" ]; then
+        print_error "Release is missing SHA256SUMS or SHA256SUMS.sig assets."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local checksums_path="$tmp_dir/SHA256SUMS"
+    local signature_path="$tmp_dir/SHA256SUMS.sig"
+
+    print_info "Downloading checksums and signature..."
+    if ! download_file "$checksums_url" "$checksums_path"; then
+        print_error "Failed to download SHA256SUMS."
+        return 1
+    fi
+    if ! download_file "$signature_url" "$signature_path"; then
+        print_error "Failed to download SHA256SUMS.sig."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if ! command -v cosign >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        print_error "cosign is required to verify release signatures."
+        print_error "Install cosign or set CAAM_SKIP_VERIFY=1 to bypass verification."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local identity="https://github.com/${REPO_OWNER}/${REPO_NAME}/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/${version}"
+    if ! cosign verify-blob \
+        --bundle "$signature_path" \
+        --certificate-oidc-issuer "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
+        --certificate-identity "$identity" \
+        "$checksums_path" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        print_error "Signature verification failed."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [ -z "$asset_name" ]; then
+        asset_name=$(basename "$archive_path")
+    fi
+
+    local expected actual
+    expected=$(grep -F " $asset_name" "$checksums_path" | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
+    if [ -z "$expected" ]; then
+        print_error "Checksum entry not found for $asset_name."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        actual=$(sha256sum "$archive_path" | awk '{print $1}')
+    elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        actual=$(shasum -a 256 "$archive_path" | awk '{print $1}')
+    else
+        print_error "sha256sum or shasum is required for checksum verification."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [ "$expected" != "$actual" ]; then
+        print_error "Checksum verification failed."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    print_success "Verified release signature and checksum."
+    return 0
+}
+
 is_tty() {
     [ -t 0 ] && [ -t 1 ]
 }
@@ -420,6 +543,10 @@ try_binary_install() {
         return 1
     fi
 
+    if ! verify_release_assets "$release_json" "$version" "$asset_name" "$archive_path" "$tmp_dir"; then
+        return 1
+    fi
+
     print_info "Extracting..."
 
     if [[ "$ext" == ".zip" ]]; then
